channel.title: Jiǎoluò Shūwū · 角落書屋
channel.description: Notes on Chinese language and civilisation — characters, vocabulary, grammar, history, philosophy, and the world they shaped.

item.title: 楚辞 · Songs of Chu
item.description: The shamanistic southern voice that gave Chinese literature its second pole — visionary, romantic, and inconsolably exiled.

item.title: 昆曲 · Kun Opera
item.description: The oldest surviving form of Chinese opera — refined to such perfection over four centuries that it nearly disappeared from its own beauty.

item.title: 鲁迅 · Lu Xun
item.description: The writer who looked at traditional Chinese society and saw cannibalism — and the complicated, unresolvable legacy he left behind.

item.title: 诗经 · Book of Songs
item.description: China oldest poetry anthology — three hundred poems that Confucius said no educated person could afford to ignore.

item.title: 水墨画 · Ink-Wash Painting
item.description: Where the brush meets philosophy — five tones of ink, the art of leaving space, and landscapes that are not about landscape at all.

item.title: 宋词 · Song Dynasty Lyric
item.description: The poem that sings — fixed tune patterns, named meters, and three masters who defined what the Chinese lyric could feel.

item.title: 文房四宝 · Four Treasures of the Study
item.description: Brush, ink, inkstone, and paper — the four instruments through which a scholar thought, wrote, painted, and demonstrated who he was.

item.title: 篆刻 · Seal Carving
item.description: The art of the name — bronze to stone, dynasty to dynasty, two schools of carvers, and the small red square that authenticates everything.

item.title: 爱 · love, to love, to cherish
item.description: Love in all its forms — romantic, familial, and the Confucian ethic of universal benevolence.

item.title: 不 · no, not, negation
item.description: The great negator — the most used character in Mandarin and the foundation of all denial.

item.title: 吃 · to eat
item.description: To eat — and the extraordinary range of Chinese life encoded in a single food verb.

item.title: 打 · to hit, to strike; the most versatile verb in Mandarin
item.description: To hit — and a hundred extensions: make a call, play a sport, pack a bag, get an injection. The most productive light verb in Chinese.

item.title: 大 · big, great, large
item.description: Big, great, large — a person with arms wide open, the original measure of magnitude.

item.title: 佛 · Buddha, the awakened
item.description: The character that carried an entire worldview across the Silk Road — etymology, Buddhism, the 佛-family of compounds.

item.title: 福 · fortune, blessing, good luck
item.description: The most recognized Chinese character in the world — fortune, blessing, and the art of the upside-down 福.

item.title: 国 · country, nation, state
item.description: A walled enclosure protecting its people — the Chinese concept of nationhood written in a single character.

item.title: 好 · good; to like
item.description: Good, well, to like — and the tone split that doubles its expressive range.

item.title: 角 · corner, horn, angle
item.description: Corner, horn, angle — the character that opens this site's name: 角落書屋.

item.title: 落 · to fall, to settle, to land
item.description: Falling, settling, and the beauty of things arriving at their place.

item.title: 美 · beautiful, USA, delicious
item.description: A large sheep carrying horns — beauty as abundance, sacrifice, and the pleasure of the highest good.

item.title: 明 · bright, clear, tomorrow
item.description: Sun and moon combined — brightness, clarity, dawn, and understanding.

item.title: 木 · tree, wood
item.description: A tree seen whole: trunk, branches, roots. The pictograph that became a radical, a wuxing element, and the building block of 林, 森, 本, and 末.

item.title: 钱 · money, coin, currency
item.description: Money — the character that drives markets, proverbs, and the Chinese conversation about wealth.

item.title: 山 · mountain, hill, sacred peak
item.description: Three peaks rising from the earth — the oldest pictograph and the axis of Chinese sacred geography.

item.title: 生 · life, birth, raw, student
item.description: A sprouting plant — life emerging from the earth. The most generative radical in Chinese.

item.title: 书 · book, writing, letter
item.description: Book, writing, calligraphy — the character that holds civilization together.

item.title: 水 · water
item.description: The first of the five elements, the radical that runs through hundreds of compounds, and the Daoist image of how to act in the world.

item.title: 说 · to speak, to say, to explain
item.description: To speak, say, explain, and persuade — the character at the center of all communication.

item.title: 王 · king
item.description: Three horizontals joined by one vertical — the ruler who connects heaven, earth, and humanity. Also the most common Chinese surname, and a radical that is secretly 玉 (jade) in disguise.

item.title: 屋 · room, house, dwelling
item.description: Room, dwelling, and the human need for shelter — the character at home.
